title: Helia macrantha
taxonomy: Helia macrantha is classified as a species of flowering plant within the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Helia, which is part of the larger family of Rubiaceae. The genus Helia is a group of flowering plants known to be native to tropical regions. The exact classification of Helia macrantha may vary depending on the source. Further research may be necessary to determine its classification in more detail.
uses: Helia macrantha has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its roots and leaves are said to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antioxidant and anti-oxidative effects. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Helia macrantha's medicinal uses.
